stories.title: EU approves €90bn loan for Ukraine and fresh Russia sanctions – as it happened
stories.summary: Move comes after Hungary and Slovakia dropped opposition following reopening of the Druzhba oil pipeline Meanwhile, Ukraine’s president Volodymyr Zelenskiy said that Kyiv will seek to receive the first tranche of the €90bn European Union loan by the end of May, or early June. “This is strengthening of our army,” he told reporters in a WhatsApp chat, reported by Reuters. Continue reading...

stories.title: The Guardian view on Anthropic’s Claude Mythos: when AI finds every flaw, who controls the internet? | Editorial
stories.summary: Tech can scale cyber-attacks and defences alike, raising questions about private power, public risk and the future of a shared internet Anthropic announced its latest AI model, Claude Mythos , this month but said it would not be released publicly, because it turns computers into crime scenes. The company claimed that it could find previously unknown “zero-day” flaws, exploit them and, in principle, link these weaknesses in order to take over major operating systems and web browsers . Mythos did

stories.title: EU forges ahead with membership for Ukraine and Moldova after Orbán’s exit
stories.summary: AYIA NAPA, Cyprus — Ukraine and Moldova will be able to move forward with the process of joining the EU following Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán’s election defeat, diplomats and officials said, ending a four-year stalemate over their applications. The two countries have seen their path to membership delayed after being granted candidate status in 2022, with Orbán vetoing the formal initiation of negotiating “clusters” — the groups of reforms and undertakings candidate countries have to ac

stories.title: After toasting €90B loan, EU leaders face up to the morning after
stories.summary: AYIA NAPA, Cyprus ― The afternoon began with the EU finally sealing its €90 billion loan to Ukraine. The evening ended with leaders confronting the sheer scale of everything else they have to do. Among other things: Get Kyiv into the EU, beef up the bloc’s mutual defense clause, shield themselves from the economic fallout of the war in Iran and sort out their collective €1.8 trillion budget for another seven years. Sounds easy, right? As European heads of state and government dined together on T

stories.title: Despite the warm sea breeze, icy winds blast Europe’s leaders
stories.summary: AYIA NAPA, Cyprus — In the glass pavilion by the sea, EU leaders found themselves reaching for extra layers when it got chilly as their conversation stretched late into the evening. Shielding Europe from the winds blowing across the continent is a taller order. Despite Thursday’s scheduled short working dinner turning into a roughly five-hour marathon, the EU’s presidents and prime ministers are no closer to working out how to respond to the world’s upheavals — or even whether they should. Remin

stories.title: MAGA’s Romanian champion George Simion eyes comeback as government implodes
stories.summary: Hard-right firebrand George Simion lost Romania’s presidential race last year , but as the country’s centrist government coalition crumbles, he now sees an alternative route to power.   Romania’s mainstream pro-EU parties are scrambling to keep the ultranationalist and euroskeptic Simion on the sidelines while they negotiate potential resolutions to the crisis. But the MAGA-aligned politician is way ahead in national polls, and insists it’s time his party had a shot at gov

stories.title: Trump got her out of a KGB prison. Now she needs him to help free her husband.
stories.summary: Few people experience such a sudden reversal in their lives: from being held captive in a KGB prison by one of the world’s most brutal regimes to freedom in Europe. Journalist Katsiaryna Andreeva is one of them. Arrested in 2020 for filming a protest against Belarusian strongman Alexander Lukashenko from a 14th-floor window, Andreeva was first sentenced to two years in prison on charges of “violating public order.” In July 2022, she was hit with a second charge, treason, and condemned to another

stories.title: EU formally approves €90bn Ukraine loan and 20th sanctions package against Russia
stories.summary: Ursula von der Leyen hails ‘good news’ after Hungary’s lifting of vetoes allows leaders to sign off on agreements EU leaders have welcomed the end of diplomatic deadlock over a long-awaited €90bn (£78bn) loan for Ukraine, after the bloc completed the agreement along with a 20th sanctions package against Russia. After weeks of delay, the EU signed off on the loan on Thursday, in time for a summit in Cyprus that began in the evening and will include talks over a dinner with the Ukrainian leader, V

stories.title: Woman killed by bear in Polish forest, son and local government say
stories.summary: In an extremely rare fatal encounter, the 58-year-old was killed as she was walking in a mountainous area, her son told rescuers.

stories.title: Newsletter: EU leaders weigh a NATO-style common defence clause
stories.summary: Also in this newsletter: EU unblocks €90bn Ukraine loan and new Russia sanctions.

stories.title: Egy évtizede gyűrik, a más uniós országokban dolgozó magyarok jogairól is szól egy új alku
stories.summary: Két bedőlt egyezség után ismét megállapodtak a szociális jogokról szóló javaslatról. Az általában a keleti munkaerőnél felmerülő „szociális dömping” ellen is fellépnének.

stories.title: Hat kategóriadíjat és a nagydíjat is elnyerték a Telex fotósai a Magyar Sajtófotó Pályázaton
stories.summary: Melegh Noémi Napsugár több díj mellett az André Kertész Nagydíjat is elhozta, Bődey Jánost is kétszer díjazták.

stories.title: Ezek voltak 2025 legjobb magyar sajtófotói
stories.summary: Halmozottan sérült, autizmussal élő kisgyerekről készült sorozat, és az éneklő Magyar Péterről készült fotó is nyert az idei, sorban a 44. Sajtófotó Pályázaton, ahol a Telex fotósait is bőségesen díjazták.

stories.title: A magyarok 1945 óta törvénytelenül használják a földeket, mondta a szlovák kormány alelnöke
stories.summary: Robert Kaliňák a jövőbeni magyar-szlovák viszony egyik legkényesebb pontjáról, a Beneš-dekrétumokról beszélt.

stories.title: Szabó Bence panaszt nyújtott be az elbocsátása miatt
stories.summary: A Magyar Helsinki Bizottság szerint Szabó Bence jogviszonyát szabályellenesen, anyagi és erkölcsi sérelmet okozva szüntette meg a rendőrség.

stories.title: Újabb minisztereket jelent be pénteken Magyar Péter
stories.summary: Köztük lesz a leendő oktatási miniszter is. Eddig kilenc minisztert jelentett be a hamarosan felálló Tisza-kormányból.

stories.title: Önök milliárdosok? – a Szijjártó-interjú legerősebb pillanatai
stories.summary: Az oroszokkal való „pragmatikus viszony”, KGBéla elengedése, a felesége több százmilliós nyereségű cége, Mészáros Lőrinc, jachtozás Szíjj Lászlóval és a személyes jövője a Fideszben. A Szijjártó Péterrel készült több mint háromórás interjúnk legfontosabb pillanatai.

stories.title: Ukrajna a fronton megállította az orosz gőzhengert, de a napi működéshez égető szüksége van az uniós hitelre
stories.summary: A Barátság olajvezeték megnyitásával kötötte össze a leköszönő Orbán-kormány a 90 milliárd eurós uniós hitelt, amit keddtől nem blokkol tovább. Az utolsó előtti pillanatban érkezhet meg a pénz, hogy ne kelljen áthidaló kölcsönt felvenniük az ukránoknak. A Tisza-kormány új lapot nyithat a magyar–ukrán viszonyban, de maradnak régi kérdések. Rácz Andrással elemeztük az elmúlt időszak történéseit.

stories.title: „Hosszú időre túszul ejtett minket” – búcsúztak az EU-csúcsot kihagyó Orbántól az uniós kollégái
stories.summary: Belgium miniszterelnöke szerint egy kicsit túl nagy az eufória a magyar kormányfő hiánya miatt, és arra utalt, hogy Magyar Péterrel se feltétlenül lesz könnyű mindenben megegyezni.

stories.title: Ütött már el szarvast?
stories.summary: Az ország több részén megnőtt az esély, hogy fog. Becslések szerint 250 ezer példány flangál Magyarországon, ami rengeteg bajt és kockázatot jelent. Az erdészek és a vadászok a probléma mértékében és kezelési módjában sem értenek egyet.

stories.title: Zelenszkij: Orbánnak nem volt igaza, nem szabad szítani a gyűlöletet a szomszédaink iránt, látjuk az eredményt
stories.summary: Békében kell élnünk – üzente Ukrajna elnöke a magyaroknak az EU-csúcsról, amire ő elment, a magyar kormányfő nem.

stories.title: A Tisza kampányfőnöke: 2022-ben is nyerhetett volna az ellenzék, de Márki-Zay nem kért a tanácsából
stories.summary: Tóth Péter szerint az volt a különbség a 2022-es összellenzék és a 2024-es EP-s Tisza-eredmények között, hogy előbbi két nap alatt égett el, utóbbi fenntartotta és növelte bázisát. Arról is beszélt, hogy szerinte Magyar Péter miniszterelnökként sem hagyhatja abba az országjárást.

stories.title: ByeAlex újra mentor lesz az X-Faktorban
stories.summary: A Solére művésznéven alkotó Szigeti Zsófia is új szereplő lesz a mentorcsapatban.

stories.title: Mit tehet egy jogállam, ha a közpénz elveszti közvagyon jellegét?
stories.summary: Az, hogy a magyar társadalom képes lesz-e a jogállami normákat betartva leszámolni a lopással és a korrupcióval, generációkra meghatározhatja az ország sorsát.

stories.title: A gyermekvonatok, amikkel egy évszázada hatvanezer gyerek utazott Magyarországról Nyugat-Európába
stories.summary: Pontosan száz éve tért haza a századik gyermekvonat Antwerpenből. Az első világháború után traumatizált gyerekeket fogadtak be holland, belga, svájci családok, általában átmenetileg, de olykor végleg.

stories.title: Szoboszlai Dominikot ötödször választották a hónap legjobb játékosának a Liverpool szurkolói
stories.summary: Február után márciusban is a magyar válogatott csapatkapitánya kapta a díjat.

stories.title: Szabó Andrea: A Fidesz több politikai hibát is vétett, Orbán az elgyurcsányosodás útjára léphet
stories.summary: A szociológus szerint a kormánypártnak nem lett volna szabad megpróbálnia kivonni az országot Európából, nyitnia kellett volna a fiatalok felé, és el kéne engednie a kirekesztő retorikát.

stories.title: Már a választás előtt csaknem ötéves csúcsra ugrott a magyarok gazdasági optimizmusa
stories.summary: A GKI üzleti és fogyasztói bizalmi indexe is nőni tudott április elején, a rossz hír csak az, hogy a felmérés szerint rengeteg cég készül éppen árat emelni.

stories.title: Egyhangúlag elfogadták az ukrán hitelhez hiányzó jogszabályt az EU kormányai
stories.summary: Az egyhangúlag jóváhagyott írásbeli eljárásban senki sem tiltakozott 24 órán belül, így elfogadták a jogszabályt, amelyet a magyar kormány az ukrán kőolajtranzit újraindulásáig blokkolt.

stories.title: Romániában életbe lép Európa legátfogóbb nőgyilkosság-törvénye
stories.summary: A jogszabály a tervek szerint a büntetés mellett a megelőzésre is hangsúlyt fektet majd.

stories.title: Kiléptek a szociáldemokrata miniszterek a román kormányból, hivatalosan is válságban a koalíció
stories.summary: A kormányzásról nem mondana le a párt, csak a miniszterelnököt távolítanák el.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: A képviselőink nem költöznek be az ÁVH egykori épületébe, a Képviselői Irodaházba
stories.summary: A leendő miniszterelnök azt is közölte, hogy meg fogják nyitni az ügynökaktákat.

stories.title: Egyhangúlag elfogadták a 20. szankciócsomagot az uniós kormányok
stories.summary: A magyar és a szlovák kormány az ukrán kőolajtranzit újraindulása miatt blokkolt, így csak két hónappal a tervezett határidő után tudták elfogadni az új büntetőintézkedéseket.

stories.title: Segít az erős forint, egyelőre olcsóbbak a nyári repülőjegyek, mint tavaly
stories.summary: A drága kerozin miatt egyre több légitársaság ritkítja járathálózatát, ugyanakkor a Magyarországról induló rövid távú nyári járatok többsége idén még mindig olcsóbb, mint tavaly.

stories.title: Egykori MNB-alelnök: Simor Andrásnak az Erstében betöltött tisztségével összefüggő döntésekre nekem nincs és nem is volt ráhatásom
stories.summary: A Magyar Nemzeti Bank közölte, hogy vizsgálatot indít a kérdéses időszakra vonatkozóan.

stories.title: Bányafelügyeleti eljárás indult Orbán Viktor testvérének és anyjának közös cége ellen
stories.summary: A kitermelési mennyiségek miatt indul vizsgálat a Hahót Tőzeg Kft.-vel szemben.

stories.title: Átlépte az egymillió megtekintést a Szijjártó Péterrel készített interjúnk
stories.summary: A leköszönő külgazdasági és külügyminisztert élőben, több mint három órán keresztül kérdezte Fábián Tamás.

stories.title: It took a massive propaganda campaign to turn Hungarians into potato lovers
stories.summary: Potatoes are such an important part of Hungarian cuisine that it is hard to imagine that as recently as two hundred years ago, people did not want to eat them.

stories.title: Ukraine says Russian oil to Hungary, Slovakia has restarted
stories.summary: Energy companies in Ukraine and Hungary have both said that oil flows through the Druzhba pipeline from Russia via Belarus have restarted. Hungary and Slovakia expect the first supplies to arrive by Thursday.

stories.title: EU unblocks €90 billion loan to Ukraine
stories.summary: Envoys from EU member states have finally paved the way for a €90 billion loan to Ukraine that had long been blocked by Hungary. They also approved a 20th package of sanctions on Russia over its invasion of its neighbor.

stories.title: EU approves €90bn loan for Ukraine as pipeline is turned on ending deadlock
stories.summary: Ukraine says it has reopened the Druzhba pipeline, after months of stalemate over stalled oil supplies to neighbouring Hungary.

stories.title: Nine lessons for the US from Viktor Orbán’s defeat | Kenneth Roth
stories.summary: As US elections approach, the Hungarian prime minister’s loss is a reminder that history does not march relentlessly toward autocracy Viktor Orbán’s electoral loss was a slap in the face for Donald Trump and JD Vance, who had enthusiastically endorsed Europe’s most visible autocrat but proved unable to salvage his candidacy. But Hungarian voters’ 12 April rejection of Orbán also holds important lessons for Americans who hope to resist Trump’s own autocratic tendencies. As the November midterm el

stories.title: Hungary: Orban-era LGBTQ law infringes human rights, ECJ rules
stories.summary: The EU's top court has found that Hungary's 2021 laws on issues including transgender and homosexual identity and sex offenses breach various EU laws. This includes Article 2 of EU Treaty, to protect human dignity.

stories.title: Rat poison found in baby food, extortion suspected
stories.summary: Rat poison has been found in HiPP baby food jars across Austria, Czechia and Slovakia in what authorities suspect is a blackmail attempt.

stories.title: Ukraine hits Russian Black Sea port city, 1 dead
stories.summary: Russian authorities said one man was killed in a Ukrainian drone raid on city of Tuapse. Meanwhile, Russia arrested a German woman in an alleged bomb plot.

stories.title: Extended naval blockade is admission US military escalation poses even greater risk
stories.summary: Battle of the blockades may still have more time to run as the US and Iran try to assert control over the strait of Hormuz Donald Trump’s decision to extend the naval blockade of Iran indefinitely may do nothing to reduce world oil prices – but it could amount to a recognition that further US military escalation in breach of the nominal ceasefire comes with greater risk against a regime disinclined to surrender. In theory, Trump’s military options are increasing. A third US carrier strike group,

stories.title: MEPs eye hefty EU budget increase for health
stories.summary: Members of the European Parliament are pushing for more than €20 billion in health funding in the next EU budget, significantly increasing the existing budget for health care projects. The European Parliament’s health committee wants to draw a clear line between funding for public health policies, such as improving cancer care, and projects to boost industries, including the pharmaceutical and medical devices sectors. In addition, the lawmakers want funds designated for defense and digital purpo

stories.title: Israeli killing of Lebanese journalist draws international condemnation
stories.summary: Lebanese PM calls attack that killed Amal Khalil a ‘war crime’, with rescuers attempting to free her also targeted Middle East crisis – live updates Israel’s killing of a prominent Lebanese journalist in a double-tap strike has been greeted with international outrage as Lebanon’s prime minister described the attack as a “war crime”. Amal Khalil , 43, who worked for al-Akhbar newspaper, was buried on Thursday. She was killed in what colleagues described as a sustained attack by Israeli forces, wi

stories.title: Surrey police to close rape inquiry that sparked Epsom disorder
stories.summary: Force says it is ‘confident there was no offence’ and condemns ‘shameful’ behaviour by protesters The investigation into reports of a rape outside a church in Epsom that led to widespread public disorder will close as police are “confident there was no offence”. Surrey police received a report on Saturday 11 April that a woman had been raped near a church in the early hours of the morning after leaving Labyrinth nightclub in Epsom. Continue reading...

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Trump bristles at Prince Harry’s passionate plea for Ukraine
stories.summary: Duke of Sussex says US must ‘honour obligations’ as it persuaded Ukraine to give up nuclear weapons; US president retorts that Harry doesn’t speak for Britain. What we know on day 1,521 Donald Trump has said the Duke of Sussex “is not speaking for the UK” after Prince Harry told the US to honour its obligations in the Ukrainian conflict . “I think I am speaking for the UK more than Prince Harry … But I appreciate his advice very much,” said Trump, responding to the duke’s lengthy, impassioned sp

stories.title: US soldier involved in Maduro raid charged over alleged bets on capture
stories.summary: Gannon Ken Van Dyke, who allegedly made more than $400,000 on Polymarket, could face up to 60 years in prison A US soldier who played a role in the January capture of Venezuelan president Nicolás Maduro is now in custody after allegedly cashing in over $400,000 on wagers about the politician’s removal from office, federal authorities announced on Thursday. Prosecutors say beginning in early December the soldier, Gannon Ken Van Dyke, was involved in planning for the military operation to capture

stories.title: ‘Hairdryer or lighter?’: French police look at claim of sensor tampering to win weather bets
stories.summary: Forecasting service raises alarm over data from Paris airport used to settle Polymarket wagers on temperature French police are investigating alleged tampering with national weather forecasting service equipment after a series of unusual temperature readings coincided with suspicious winning bets made on Polymarket. Data from a Météo-France weather station at Paris’s Charles de Gaulle airport was used to settle bets between online gamblers on what the temperature would be in Paris for March and

stories.title: Foreign Office unit tracking Israel’s potential breaches of international law closes due to cuts
stories.summary: Exclusive: Officials warn department will also lose access to database of 26,000 verified incidents due to cuts The Foreign Office unit tracking potential breaches of international law by Israel in Gaza and more recently Lebanon has been closed because of cuts within the department, the Guardian can reveal. The decision to shut the international humanitarian law cell follows a review by Olly Robbins, the permanent secretary at the Foreign Office dismissed last week by the prime minister over the

stories.title: Congress members join protest against ‘oligarch’s dinner’ for Trump thrown by Ellisons
stories.summary: Paramount Skydance CEO fetes administration as it weighs $110bn merger with CNN parent WarnerBros Discovery Dozens of protesters, including members of Congress, gathered along the National Mall on Thursday to protest an “intimate” dinner being held by Paramount Skydance chief executive David Ellison “in celebration of the First Amendment” and “honoring the Trump White House and CBS White House Correspondents”, and attended by Donald Trump . Paramount has faced criticism for the dinner, which has

stories.title: ‘If you buy Kyle, you get Kyle’: Sandilands’ lawyer defends sacked Kiis FM shock jock in Sydney court
stories.summary: Radio presenter arrives by Rolls-Royce for case management hearing into allegations he breached his radio contract Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Kyle Sandilands’ conduct may not be “nice” but “if you buy Kyle, you get Kyle”, the federal court has heard in a high-profile legal fight the sacked shock jock describes as “pretty ugly”. Sandilands’ barrister, Scott Robertson SC, has told the court the allegations abo

stories.title: Former inspector general urges audit of Epstein files release be conducted ‘without undue influence’ – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. DoJ inspector general to audit department’s compliance with Epstein Files Transparency Act Sign up for the Breaking News US email You’ve likely seen that the Senate adopted the plan for the budget blueprint for ICE and border patrol after an all-night “vote-a-rama”. This is, in fact, not a congressional dance break. Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump claims US has total control over strait of Hormuz after Iran seizes two container ships
stories.summary: US president says Tehran hobbled by infighting as Pentagon reportedly briefs mine clearance may take six months Middle East crisis – live updates Donald Trump has again said that the US has “total control over the strait of Hormuz”, adding that Iran’s leadership was so hobbled by infighting that it was unclear who was in charge. But the US president’s claim seemed questionable in the face of the seizure of two container ships by Iranian commandos and a US report warning it could take six months

stories.title: Trump says Israel and Lebanon agree to extend ceasefire by three weeks – Middle East crisis live
stories.summary: US president says meeting between Israel’s and Lebanon’s ambassadors to the United States went ‘very well’ The Pentagon abruptly announced that the secretary of the US navy, John Phelan, would be leaving his job yesterday. No reason was given for the unexpected departure of the navy’s top civilian official, who had addressed a large crowd of sailors and industry professionals at the navy’s annual conference in Washington just a day before the announcement. People familiar with the dynamics at th

stories.title: News live: Pocock accuses Labor of ‘ignoring’ vulnerable Australians in favour of gas companies as report calls for jobseeker rise
stories.summary: Follow today’s news live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Allegra Spender says 25% gas export tax would help fix ‘faulty’ system Independent MP Allegra Spender said a 25% tax on gas exports would help rectify what she sees as “faulty” taxation arrangements that have seen an Australian resource sent overseas with minimal benefit to the country. The gas industry is a very profitable industry and pays income tax. And every company in Australia, frankly, should pay income

stories.title: ICC says Philippines' Duterte can stand trial over war on drugs
stories.summary: The ICC has said there is enough evidence linking former Philippines president Rodrigo Duterte to crimes against humanity over his role in orchestrating a deadly anti-drugs crackdown.

stories.title: Germany news: Bavarian police arrest suspected spies
stories.summary: Two men have been arrested in Bavaria and charged with espionage, Frankfurt Airport's new Terminal 3 has opened its doors, and former US President Obama has praised a German climate activist. DW has more.

stories.title: More than 500 people killed in Tanzania election violence, inquiry finds
stories.summary: Opposition parties accused security forces but the official report does not say who was responsible.

stories.title: Israel awaiting US green light to 'return Iran to Stone Age,' defence minister says
stories.summary: The war has spilled out of Iran and engulfed the region, leaving several thousand people dead, mainly in Iran and Lebanon, and continues to destabilise the global economy.

stories.title: Lebanon accuses Israel of targeting journalist killed in air strike
stories.summary: Lebanon's prime minister accused Israel of war crimes after IDF attacks on Red Cross vehicles also stopped rescuers from reaching the site.

stories.title: Is the tide turning for Ukraine in war with Russia? – The Latest
stories.summary: With the EU approving a €90bn loan for Ukraine, a surprise visit from Prince Harry, and data suggesting Russian troops made almost no territorial gains in March – are there reasons for optimism in Kyiv? Lucy Hough speaks to senior international correspondent Luke Harding – watch on YouTube Continue reading...

stories.title: Dozens of Mexican mafia members arrested in California crackdown
stories.summary: Prosecutors say 43 people indicted on charges including murder, kidnapping, extortion and drug trafficking More than two dozen members and associates of the Mexican mafia were arrested during an early morning crackdown in southern California, federal authorities said on Thursday. The FBI and other federal and local agencies executed search and arrest warrants at locations mostly in Orange county, south of Los Angeles, according to the US attorney’s office. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK prepared to deploy RAF Typhoons to keep strait of Hormuz open after Iran war
stories.summary: Proposal at heart of offer made during a 30-country two-day meeting jointly organised by France Middle East crisis – live updates Britain is prepared to deploy a squadron of RAF Typhoons based in Qatar to patrol over the strait of Hormuz as part of a multinational mission to keep open the strategic waterway once the Iran war comes to an end. The UK military also offered to deploy mine-hunting drones and specialist divers to help clear the strait mined by Iran – but no decision has been made on w

stories.title: Baby died after NHS trust failed to warn mother of ‘unsafe’ home birth, coroner finds
stories.summary: Seven-day-old Poppy Hope Lomas died after complications during home birth encouraged by midwives at Barnet hospital A mother who lost her baby a week after an “unsafe” home birth that went against medical advice was failed by the NHS, an inquest has found. Poppy Hope Lomas was seven days old when she died at University College hospital in London on 26 October 2022 after complications during a home birth that, according to her mother, was encouraged by midwives at Barnet hospital. Continue readin

stories.title: ‘Toxic’ views of Reform UK candidates raise questions about party’s vetting
stories.summary: Hope Not Hate campaign identifies election hopefuls calling for a ‘white Britain’ and complaining of ‘kowtowing to the black community’ A Reform UK candidate who called for a “white Britain” and said Keir Starmer should be shot is among a number of contenders fuelling doubts about the party’s claim to have tightened up its vetting. The past comments of Linda McFarlane and other political hopefuls have been unearthed ahead of the 7 May elections, including one who complained about “constant kowto

stories.title: Trump may talk of regime infighting, but Iran seems united by strategy born of war
stories.summary: Tensions around US negotiations may reflect mistake of assassinating more pragmatic and experienced figures Middle East crisis – live updates Donald Trump has claimed that the infighting between moderates and hardliners in Iran’s leadership is so intense that Iranians have “no idea who their leader is”, but many experts questioned his analysis, saying, given the mass assassinations of senior commanders , the country had shown remarkable institutional cohesion. Trump’s allegations of “CRAZY” spli

stories.title: India voices anger after Trump shares comments calling it a ‘hellhole’
stories.summary: Foreign ministry says remarks of conservative podcast host Michael Savage that were shared by US president were ‘obviously uninformed, inappropriate and in poor taste’ India on Thursday criticised as inappropriate a post by Donald Trump in which he shared comments that called the South Asian country a “hellhole”. The inflammatory post on Truth Social comes ahead of a planned visit next month to India by the US secretary of state, Marco Rubio, who is seeking to ease recent tensions between the no

stories.title: Trump says he will ‘probably put a big tariff on the UK’ if it doesn’t drop digital services tax
stories.summary: US president accuses UK of thinking it can ‘make an easy buck’ from US tech companies, weeks after warning that UK–US trade deal can be changed Donald Trump has threatened to impose tariffs on the UK if it does not drop its digital services tax on US social media firms. The digital services tax, introduced in 2020, imposes a 2% levy on the revenues of several major US tech companies. Continue reading...

stories.title: The international right has CPAC. Has the left finally found its answer? | Owen Jones
stories.summary: Spain’s PM, Pedro Sánchez, hosted the inaugural meeting of the Global Progressive Mobilisation. Keir Starmer and other social democrats were notable by their absence Can progressives push back the rising tide of authoritarianism? Thousands of people gathered in Barcelona this weekend in search of an answer. The occasion was the inaugural Global Progressive Mobilisation – an ambitious initiative backed by the Spanish prime minister, Pedro Sánchez – which drew an impressive cast: Brazil’s Lula, Me

stories.title: Bibby Stockholm asylum barge contractor admits overcharging UK government £118m
stories.summary: Australia’s Corporate Travel Management is ‘negotiating commercial arrangements’ to refund the money The Australian company that ran the Bibby Stockholm asylum barge has admitted it overcharged the British government by £118m. Corporate Travel Management (CTM) said its auditor had found evidence of “erroneous billing” of its UK clients, increasing its estimate of how much it owes the government by £40m. Continue reading...

stories.title: Philippines’ ex-president Rodrigo Duterte to face trial for crimes against humanity
stories.summary: ICC judges say there are substantial grounds to believe Duterte guided anti-drugs crackdown that killed thousands The former president of the Philippines, Rodrigo Duterte , will face trial at the international criminal court (ICC) after judges unanimously confirmed charges of crimes against humanity over his “war on drugs”. Pre-trial judges concluded on Thursday that there were substantial grounds to believe Duterte was responsible for the crimes against humanity of murder and attempted murder i

stories.title: Germany unveils first ever military strategy for Bundeswehr
stories.summary: For the first time in its history, the Bundeswehr has adopted a military strategy to prepare Germany for future threats. Large sections of the document are classified.

stories.title: UK: Health data volunteered by 500,000 people to Biobank charity listed for sale on Alibaba in China
stories.summary: The British government is investigating how health data volunteered to a charity for research purposes wound up for sale via three vendors on the Alibaba e-commerce site in China. One listing had 500,000 people's data.

stories.title: Samsung workers rally, call for larger share of AI profits
stories.summary: Unions at Samsung are threatening a weekslong strike if demands are not met. A production halt at one of the world's largest memory chipmakers could disrupt global chip supplies.

stories.title: Is Ethiopia bracing for war?
stories.summary: Analysts say the war in Iran has temporarily delayed an escalation in smoldering tensions between Ethiopia and Eritrea. But renewed armed conflict cannot be ruled out.

stories.title: Ceasefire extended: What's next in the Iran war?
stories.summary: With no fixed ceasefire deadline for now, the US and Iran are looking for ways to safeguard their own interests. DW takes a look at the main sticking points and explains some key issues.

stories.title: What is Europe's plan to secure the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.summary: France and the UK are leading efforts to establish a multinational mission to safeguard shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Questions remain about the scope of the operation and whether it can bring lasting stability.

stories.title: Warner Bros shareholders greenlight sale to Paramount
stories.summary: The deal would combine two major Hollywood studios and bring CBS and CNN under one roof, tightening an already concentrated media landscape, critics say.

stories.title: Trump says Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended by three weeks
stories.summary: Donald Trump's remarks follow a meeting at the White House with high-ranking officials from Israel and Lebanon. The extension comes after Israel struck southern Lebanon and Hezbollah fired rockets at Israel. Follow DW.

stories.title: Reza Pahlavi calls for regime change in Iran on Berlin visit
stories.summary: The former Shah's son is in Germany to drum up support for his global campaign to overthrow Iran's leadership. During a speech in the capital, he called the German government's refusal to meet with him "a disgrace."

stories.title: Pope Leo stressed importance of dialogue on Africa trip
stories.summary: During a visit to four African countries, Pope Leo XIV called for peace, unity and dialogue. He criticized war, corruption and inequality, urging leaders to put people first.

stories.title: US-Kuwaiti journalist held in Kuwait over social media posts acquitted, lawyers say
stories.summary: Ahmed Shihab-Eldin reportedly faced charges, including spreading false information, after he re-posted images related to the Iran war.

stories.title: South African police chief suspended over $20m health contract
stories.summary: Cyril Ramaphosa said the suspension was because of the critical role Fannie Masemola plays in fighting crime.

stories.title: Warner Bros shareholders approve Paramount's $111bn takeover
stories.summary: The approval came as Donald Trump is to attend a dinner with billionaire Paramount backers the Ellisons.

stories.title: US still delivering weapons to Ukraine despite Iran war, Zelenskyy says
stories.summary: Meanwhile, three people were killed and 10 others wounded in a Russian drone attack on the central city of Dnipro.

stories.title: Huge chunk of glacier blocks Everest route in peak climbing season
stories.summary: Sherpas cannot prepare the route to the world's highest peak because a huge block of ice is in the way.

stories.title: UN 'working on' keeping presence in Lebanon after UNIFIL mandate expires in 2027
stories.summary: The United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon has served fulfilled peacekeeping duties between Israel and Lebanon since 1978 but finds itself caught in the crossfire between Israeli forces and Hezbollah.

stories.title: Trump tells BBC that King's visit could 'absolutely' help repair relations with UK
stories.summary: In a phone interview with the BBC's North America editor, the president discussed next week's visit and his relationship with the UK PM.

stories.title: Masked Iranian forces appear to seize ships in staged video
stories.summary: Analysis indicates that parts of the video appear to have been filmed hours after the ships were reportedly seized.

stories.title: US government watchdog to investigate Epstein files release
stories.summary: The justice department's inspector general will review the withholding and overdue release of secret files.

stories.title: US boards ship carrying Iran oil as Trump threatens mine-laying vessels
stories.summary: Central Command said it has intercepted 33 vessels since imposing a blockade on ships coming to and from Iran.

stories.title: Singer D4vd had 'significant amount' of child sex abuse images when arrested, prosecutors say
stories.summary: The Tiktok star's attorneys have maintained his innocence and vowed to "vigorously defend" him.

stories.title: Meta says it will cut 8,000 jobs as AI spending soars
stories.summary: The cuts, which employees had been expecting for weeks, will be Meta's largest layoff since 2023.

stories.title: Ringo Starr: 'I made all my mistakes on stage'
stories.summary: Beatles star reveals all about his new country album, being a Sir and coming up with the phrase A Hard Day's Night.

stories.title: A rumour, a lynching in India and a long wait for justice
stories.summary: A court has convicted 20 people for the 2018 mob lynching of two men falsely accused of kidnapping children.

stories.title: US soldier charged after winning $400,000 betting on removal of Maduro
stories.summary: Gannon Ken Van Dyke allegedly made trades on Polymarket on the basis of classified information, the justice department says.

stories.title: Headscarf with a beret: Muslim designers showcase floral dresses and boxy streetwear in Paris
stories.summary: Some young attendees say the event demonstrated a more inclusive French culture taking shape.

stories.title: White House memo claims mass AI theft by Chinese firms
stories.summary: A memo from Michael Kratsios says firms, mainly in China, are wrongfully distilling US AI models.

stories.title: Opinion: The courage to speak
stories.summary: At the Antalya Diplomacy Forum, my experience as a rabbi showed that even small moments of dialogue can begin to rebuild fragile bridges between communities.

stories.title: South African court orders Zambia to return former president's body
stories.summary: Zambia authorities and Edgar Lungu's family are in a dispute over where the former leader's remains should be buried.

stories.title: France holds national tribute for soldier killed in Lebanon
stories.summary: The Minister for the Armed Forces, Catherine Vautrin, the Chief of Staff of the Army and General Pierre Schill preside over a national tribute ceremony in Montauban to honour the memory of Staff Sergeant Florian Montorio.

stories.title: Kazakhstan confirms suspension of its oil transit to Germany via Russia
stories.summary: Kazakhstan’s energy minister announced Wednesday there would be no transit of its oil to Germany through Russia throughout May due to Russian technical constraints, assuming it is “related to recent strikes on Russian infrastructure".

stories.title: UN rights chief alarmed by number of drugs-related executions in Singapore
stories.summary: Officials in Singapore maintain that the death penalty has made it one of the world's safest cities and a government survey in 2023 showed strong public support for capital punishment for serious crimes.

stories.title: Number of billionaires globally could reach 4,000 in next five years
stories.summary: There are now 3,110 billionaires but analysis shows ‘deep structural acceleration’ in wealth creation around world The number of billionaires in the world could reach nearly 4,000 by 2031, figures suggest, as the super-rich accumulate wealth at an accelerating rate. There are now 3,110 billionaires globally, according to analysis by the estate agent Knight Frank. This is forecast to rise by 25% over the next five years, taking the total to 3,915. Continue reading...

stories.title: Pilot’s selfie led to mid-air collision in F-15K fighter jet, says South Korea’s air force
stories.summary: One pilot ordered to repay some of the $600,000 of damage caused by collision in 2021 South Korea’s air force has apologised for a 2021 mid-air collision involving two fighter jets, a day after auditors said pilots were taking selfies and filming during the flight and held them responsible for the accident. “We sincerely apologise to the public for the concern caused by the accident that occurred in 2021,” an air force spokesperson said in a press briefing. The spokesperson said one of the pilot

stories.title: St Vincent and Grenadines government pauses constitutional amendment bills after public backlash
stories.summary: Bills are seeking to change section that opposition says makes Godwin Friday, a dual citizen, ineligible to be PM The St Vincent and the Grenadines government has delayed a controversial effort to amend a section of the country’s constitution that the opposition says renders the prime minister ineligible for his position in parliament. Two bills, among six listed for the parliament session on Tuesday this week, were aimed at clarifying a section of the 1979 constitution governing the citizenship

stories.title: ‘A history of the Earth’: Twelve Apostles revealed to be as old as 14m years
stories.summary: Tectonic plate movements over millions of years have lifted and tilted the layers, with records of ancient earthquakes in the rocks Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Microscopic fossils embedded in limestone have helped reveal the true age of Victoria’s Twelve Apostles as 8.6m to 14m years old. The conclave of giant golden pillars is visited by 2.8 million tourists each year, a highlight for those travelling along

stories.title: Prince Harry tells Putin 'there is still a moment to stop this war', at Kyiv conference
stories.summary: Duke of Sussex was speaking at the Kyiv Security Forum during a surprise visit to show support for Ukraine after four years of war with Russia. The prince added that it was also 'a moment for America to show that it can honour its international treaty obligations'. His last visit to the country was in September 2025, when he discussed support for war veterans Continue reading...

stories.title: Denmark: Two trains collide injuring several
stories.summary: Two trains have crashed into each other in Denmark, leaving several people critically injured, authorities have said.

stories.title: Bayern Munich on the brink of historic 'double treble'
stories.summary: With Bundesliga titles in the bag and cup finals to come, Bayern's men and women have a chance to achieve something unique in Europe. The two teams dominate in Germany and appear united in their Champions League aims.

stories.title: A booklover's dream holiday in Scotland
stories.summary: As UNESCO celebrates World Book Day on April 23, we visit a Scottish coastal town that was saved by books.

stories.title: Amnesty International paints a grim picture for human rights
stories.summary: In its 2025/2026 report, the human rights watchdog Amnesty International calls out the "predatory behavior of the powerful" and calls for action to defend the global order.

stories.title: Unpacking the 2026 Venice Biennale controversies and highlights
stories.summary: Following the return of Russia to the Biennale, the EU said it will carry out its threat to cut its funding from the international art show.

stories.title: How Ukraine is rebuilding its energy system under fire
stories.summary: Since Chernobyl, Ukraine has lived in the shadow of nuclear disaster. Now, with Russia targeting its power grid, it's facing a new nuclear risk. The response is reshaping how the world thinks about energy in wartime.

stories.title: EU leaders discuss Iran, Ukraine at Cyprus summit
stories.summary: The two days of talks started with the approval of a massive loan for Ukraine and are also expected to address the EU budget and the fallout from the conflict in the Middle East.

stories.title: Iran: Daily life shadowed by war, scarcity and fear
stories.summary: The US naval blockade aims to put pressure on the Iranian regime, but ordinary Iranians mostly feel the effects. Many of them find their lives shaped by growing exhaustion.

stories.title: Libya oscillates between cooperation and illusion
stories.summary: For the first time, representatives from both sides of Libya's political divide are participating in Flintlock, a multinational military exercise under US leadership. Is this a tentative sign of hope for reunification?

stories.title: Donald Trump says the US is in no rush to end war but 'clock is ticking' for Iran
stories.summary: Earlier in the day, Trump said he had ordered the US Navy to "shoot and kill" any boats found laying sea mines in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Two trains collide head-on in Denmark, leaving multiple injured
stories.summary: Two trains collided near Hillerød in Denmark leaving several people injured.

stories.title: US Navy chief leaving post 'effective immediately', Pentagon says
stories.summary: Reports say Phelan, whose role was largely administrative, left amid tension over US shipbuilding.

stories.title: US Navy Secretary John Phelan leaves post amid Iran war in latest Pentagon shake-up
stories.summary: The Pentagon shakeup continued as US Navy Secretary John Phelan suddenly departed during the Iran war, with Trump-backed combat veteran Hung Cao taking over temporarily.

stories.title: BBC visits migrant camp in northern France as new deal announced
stories.summary: A proposed three-year deal with Paris is aimed at stopping migrants crossing the Channel on small boats.

stories.title: Two runners help collapsed competitor finish Boston Marathon
stories.summary: Ajay Haridasse collapsed near the Boston Marathon finish line but was helped up by Robson De Oliveira of Brazil and Aaron Beggs of Britain, who supported him across the line as other runners continued the race. The dramatic moment unfolded during Monday’s race.

stories.title: In Tehran, money is short and a return to war looms over daily life
stories.summary: The BBC's Lyse Doucet reports from Iran, where the buzz of busy shopping streets masks deep uncertainty over the country's future.

stories.title: Pope Leo criticises Equatorial Guinea prisons as he winds up Africa tour
stories.summary: The Pope gives a frank and passionate speech, the likes of which have characterised his tour of Africa.

stories.title: Italian officials roundly reject proposal to replace Iran squad at 2026 World Cup
stories.summary: Italy missed out on the World Cup for the third successive time after losing a penalty shootout to Bosnia and Herzegovina in their qualifying playoff final.

stories.title: Trump administration reclassifies cannabis as less dangerous
stories.summary: Medical marijuana was moved into a lower category of drugs in order to increase research and access.

stories.title: MrBeast's company sued by ex-employee over sexual harassment claim
stories.summary: Beast Industries has strongly denied claims of sexual harassment and workplace gender bias.

stories.title: Two trains collide head-on in Denmark, leaving five critically hurt
stories.summary: The accident happened early on Thursday at a level crossing north of Copenhagen.

stories.title: US and Iran continue seizing ships in tense Hormuz standoff
stories.summary: The US and Iran continued seizing ships in the tense Hormuz standoff, with Tehran calling Washington's blockade a “ceasefire violation” and claiming it has been collecting transit fees for passage.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV ends Africa tour with open-air mass in Equatorial Guinea
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV held a mass in Malabo, marking the end of his 11-day Africa tour.

stories.title: Közgyűlést szerveztek az SZFE hallgatói, az egyetem kamerán nézte vissza, ki rakott ki erről plakátokat
stories.summary: Az egyetemi vezetés kifogásolta, hogy sem a plakátkihelyezésre nem kért senki engedélyt, sem az eseményre.

stories.summary: Az eddig beérkezett kérelmek közel felét tudták elbírálni.

stories.title: Simor András az Erstében elmondott búcsúbeszédében azt állította, hogy egy kritikus cikke miatt zsarolta az MNB
stories.summary: A 2023. decemberében megjelent cikkében a Matolcsy György-vezette MNB intézkedéseit kritizálta.

stories.title: Tóth Vera: Nem kerestem a választás óta Gabit, annyira paprikás most a hangulat
stories.summary: Az énekesnő kollektív szomorúságot érzett az országon, és sokáig fontolgatta, kiálljon-e a politikai nézeteivel. Fájó volt látnia a Megasztár lealjasodását, és eddig szerinte a tűrt kategóriában volt, mert elkönyvelték aranyos, semleges kislánynak.

stories.title: Kusza cenzúra a közmédiában, kiégés 26 évesen, kemény beszélgetés a családdal a választások előtt – erős idézetek a Tóth Vera-interjúból
stories.summary: A Tóth Verával készített interjúnk legfontosabb részleteit gyűjtöttük össze.

stories.summary: Dézsi Csaba Andrást másodszor idézték be tanúvallomást tenni. Vele, a volt alpolgármesterével és a kabinetfőnöke meghallgatásával folytatódott a GYHG-per.

stories.title: Vitézy Dávid bemutatta a pesti fonódó villamoshálózat első látványterveit
stories.summary: Elbontanák a Nyugati téri felüljárót, zöldsávot hoznának létre a Bajcsy-Zsilinszky úton, a Lehel téren pedig park lenne a villamosmegálló helyén.

stories.title: Trump a civilizációpusztulásos posztjáról: Bármit is mondok vagy teszek, úgy tűnik, nagyon jól működik
stories.summary: Az amerikai elnök szerint Irán alig várja, hogy megállapodást kössön.

stories.title: Pintér Sándor kitüntette a sofőrjét
stories.summary: A rendőr- és tűzoltónapon rendezett ünnepségen olyan állami vezetők is kitüntetést vehettek át, akik nem is ezen a területen dolgoznak.

stories.title: Trump nagyon nyomta, hogy az államok rajzolják át a választókerületeket, de újra és újra visszafelé sül el a terve
stories.summary: Új szintre emelték a gerrymanderinget az USA-ban, a republikánus államok egymás után kezdték el úgy átalakítani a körzeteket, hogy bebiztosítsák saját győzelmüket. A demokraták felvették a kesztyűt, és a héten Virginiában értek el fontos sikert.

stories.title: Mégsem jön vissza nyártól a reklámadó
stories.summary: Értelemszerűen az orosz-ukrán háború miatt.

stories.summary: Az indítványozó képviselők szerint a testület együtt tudna működni, de a polgármester diktatórikus módszerei, egyszemélyi irányítása ezt nem teszi lehetővé.

stories.title: Millions in India stripped of vote before critical state election, as government seeks to ‘purify’ electoral roll
stories.summary: Experts say Muslims and other minorities have been disproportionately deleted from the electoral roll ahead of the West Bengal elections this week Millions of people in the Indian state of West Bengal have been stripped of their vote ahead of a critical state election this week, after a controversial electoral revision described by critics as a “bloodless political genocide” and mass disenfranchisement of minorities. In West Bengal, a total of 9.1 million names have been deleted from the registe

stories.title: Heatwaves, floods and wildfires pose rising threat to democracy, report finds
stories.summary: Research shows natural hazards linked to climate crisis disrupted 23 elections in 18 countries in 2024 Democracy is under mounting threat from the climate crisis, with new analysis documenting how elections are increasingly shaped not only by political forces but also by floods, wildfires and extreme weather. At least 94 elections and referendums across 52 countries have been disrupted by climate-related impacts over the last two decades, researchers found. Continue reading...

stories.title: El Salvador holds mass trial for 486 alleged members of notorious MS-13 gang
stories.summary: Human rights groups have warned that the collective prosecutions violate due process and block defendants from accessing legal counsel A Salvadoran court on Tuesday began a collective trial of 486 alleged gang members, in one of the biggest mass trials under president Nayib Bukele’s crackdown on gang violence through controversial emergency powers. Prosecutors say the charges against alleged members of the Mara Salvatrucha gang, or MS-13, span more than 47,000 crimes committed between 2012 and 2

stories.title: Israeli ambassador: 'Only a strong Israel can achieve peace'
stories.summary: In an interview with DW, Ron Prosor, Israel's ambassador to Germany, defends his country's military strikes against Iran and Lebanon. He also advises Germany's Chancellor Friedrich Merz to show more "tact."

stories.title: Synthetic drugs and digital crime: Why Central Asia is emerging as a new frontline
stories.summary: The rise of synthetic drugs and digital trafficking methods is forcing a shift in global anti-narcotics strategies. Uzbekistan is advancing new initiatives to improve cross-border cooperation, financial oversight and public awareness.

stories.title: Iran executes former atomic agency employee amid Mossad spy claims
stories.summary: Iran executes former atomic energy employee Mehdi Farid for alleged Mossad links as rights groups decry opaque trial, coerced confessions and media blackout.

stories.title: He wasn't guilty but delays left this man jailed for five years without trial
stories.summary: At 17, Rasheed Wasiu was detained as security forces cracked down on protests against police brutality.

stories.title: Russia faked economic data to appear more resilient to its war and sanctions, intel report says
stories.summary: Swedish intelligence said Russia has been covering up a weak, debt-driven economy hit by war costs and sanctions, warning of rising inflation and banking risks.

stories.title: British woman died in Ghana trying to recoup money from scammers, inquest told
stories.summary: Janet Fordham died in crash after travelling to see man who claimed he would help to recover money from earlier scams A British woman who was scammed out of up to £1m in a string of so-called romance frauds died in a road crash after travelling to west Africa to try to recoup some of her lost fortune, an inquest in Devon has heard. Janet Fordham was cheated of her life savings and her home over a period of five years by fraudsters apparently based in the UK, Germany, the US and Ghana, the inques

stories.title: Trippon Norbert, Újpest polgármestere is kilépett a DK-ból
stories.summary: Függetlenként folytatja.

stories.title: Tavaly több mellkisebbítő beavatkozást végeztek az Egyesült Királyságban, mint mellnagyobbítást
stories.summary: Szakértők szerint a tendencia az elmozdulást tükrözi az aktívabb életmód felé.

stories.summary: Gyűjtőpontokon továbbra is elérhető lesz az ingyenes kormányközeli napilap.

stories.title: Nyomoznak ellene, a rendőrség mégis 353,7 millió forintot fizetett ki 2017 óta Balásy Gyula cégeinek
stories.summary: A Balásy-cégek a rendőrség válasza szerint kommunikációs, médiatervezési és -vásárlási feladatokat láttak el.

stories.title: Sokat elárul a bukott rezsimről, ahogy hirtelen megtáltosodott az ügyészség a gyanús ügyek kivizsgálásában
stories.summary: A választás után kapásból két olyan büntetőügyben is lépett az ügyészség, amiben eddig gyakorlatilag semmi nem történt. A NER gazdasági holdudvarában egyre nagyobb a pánik. Sok múlik azon, mennyire ijednek meg a strómanok, és mikor kezdenek el a piramis csúcsán állók ügyeiről beszélni a hatóságoknak.

stories.title: Megérkeztek az első kőolajszállítmányok az újraindított Barátság kőolajvezetéken
stories.summary: Három hónapos szünet után.

stories.summary: A FESZ szerint az új kulturális vezetésnek támaszkodnia kell a szakmai szervezetek tudására, és valódi partnerként kell bevonnia őket a döntéshozatalba.

stories.title: Videón, ahogy maszkos irániak motorcsónakkal elfoglalnak két konténerhajót a Hormuzi-szorosban
stories.summary: Az iráni Forradalmi Gárda fegyveresei létrán másztak fel az egyik hajó fedélzetére.

stories.title: Az éttermekben zabálták, a presszókban kiköpték a Mi Hazánkot a választók
stories.summary: A kávézókban a Tiszának se termett babér. Hihetnénk, hogy a Fidesz a kastélyokban tarolt, de nem!

stories.title: Az iváncsai akkugyárban történt tűz miatt eljárást indított a kormányhivatal
stories.summary: Senki nem sérült meg a gyárban, a katasztrófavédelem vizsgálja a tűzeset okait.

stories.title: Record number of immigrants reside in EU in 2025
stories.summary: The foreign‑born population of the EU grew to around 64 million in 2025. Germany remains the biggest host of immigrants in absolute terms, but some smaller nations have a much higher percentage of foreign-born residents.

stories.title: Iran war: Jet fuel crisis deepens as Lufthansa cuts flights
stories.summary: The impact of jet fuel shortages because of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz is starting to burn. Lufthansa has canceled 20,000 flights and experts warn more cancellations and price hikes are on the way.

stories.title: Could Iran war trigger bigger trade crisis than COVID?
stories.summary: As the Iran war simmers, trade experts ask whether it could reshape supply chains more deeply than COVID-19, forcing companies to rethink strategies to counter further geopolitical shocks.

stories.title: Avoid men, German police association chief tells women
stories.summary: Police association chief Dirk Peglow has stirred debate with his advice to women to avoid relationships with men for safety's sake. But his provocative tip is not without reason, as statistics show.

stories.title: The rise and fall of East Germany's Palace of the Republic
stories.summary: From 1976 through 1990, the building in East Berlin served as a symbol of the communist state's power. The "People's Palace" was controversially destroyed — and yet remains present.

stories.title: Russia to block Kazakh oil flows to Germany via key pipeline
stories.summary: Germany has confirmed that Russia will block flows of Kazakh oil to a vital refinery that supplies much of Berlin's energy needs from May 1.

stories.title: Germany's finance minister wants to scrap spousal tax splitting
stories.summary: Highly controversial, but not well understood: German Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil wants to change the tax benefits for married couples.

stories.title: Germany's Merz: Climate protection must not hold economy back
stories.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said the public would not accept climate policies that lead to deindustrialization, warning that they would hinder progress and innovation.

stories.title: UK, France agree 3‑year deal to curb Channel crossings
stories.summary: For the first time, the deal links some UK payments to France to performance in stopping migrants. Funding will be halted after one year if targets are not met.

stories.title: Iran seizes two cargo ships in Strait of Hormuz after three vessels fired on
stories.summary: The tanker seizures come after US President Donald Trump said the ceasefire with Iran was being indefinitely extended at the request of mediator Pakistan.

stories.title: Central Asian leaders gather at first ever regional climate summit to discuss water crisis
stories.summary: The Regional Ecological Summit kicks off today in Astana.

stories.title: Israelis mark Memorial Day during ongoing conflict
stories.summary: Israelis marked Memorial Day with ceremonies and informal tributes, reflecting on loss while continuing daily life amid ongoing conflict and uncertainty.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV condemns ‘lust for power’ and inequality in Equatorial Guinea
stories.summary: The Holy Father of the Catholic Church made a stop in the final leg of his four-country tour of Africa. Equatorial Guinea is a secular country, with around three-fourths of its population Catholic.

stories.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guards tighten grip on power as civilian leadership sidelined
stories.summary: Iran’s Revolutionary Guard has evolved from a theocratic wartime force into a dominant military-economic power. Over decades, it expanded regionally, reshaped Iran’s internal balance of power, now standing at the centre of a shifting political structure amid leadership changes and consolidation.

stories.title: Fed chair nominee Warsh rejects 'Trump sock puppet' label at Senate hearing
stories.summary: Kevin Warsh, the nominee for Chair of the US Federal Reserve, declared he has not pledged to lower interest rates to secure his position despite public calls from US President Donald Trump for immediate monetary easing.

stories.title: No cartels involved - but Mexico's pyramid attack prompts new concerns
stories.summary: The deadly shooting at a popular tourist site weeks before the World Cup has rocked Mexico.

stories.title: Lufthansa cuts 20,000 summer flights as fuel prices surge
stories.summary: The airline is the latest to cut flights as the US-Israel war with Iran sends jet fuel prices soaring.

stories.title: Madrid hosts Experience Macao Roadshow showcasing culture, food and tourism
stories.summary: The Experience Macao Roadshow in Madrid offered visitors interactive activities, cultural performances and gastronomy highlighting the Asian territory’s tourism appeal.

stories.title: Syria's president arrives in Qatar for talks after Saudi Arabia visit
stories.summary: Syria’s president Ahmed al-Sharaa visited Doha on Wednesday for talks with Qatar’s emir on economic cooperation, investment and post-al-Assad regional reintegration.

stories.title: US and Iran in blockade standoff as Pakistan pushes for talks
stories.summary: The mood in the Strait of Hormuz remains combustible despite Trump's ceasefire extension.

stories.title: Nigeria's suspected coup plotters deny treason charges
stories.summary: Allegations of a coup plot first surfaced last year when an Independence Day parade was cancelled.

stories.title: Aerial footage shows widespread destruction in southern Lebanon
stories.summary: A 10-day ceasefire went into effect in Lebanon on Friday, but there have been several Israeli strikes and Hezbollah claimed its first attack on Tuesday.

stories.title: Billionaire backer sues Trump family's crypto firm over alleged extortion
stories.summary: Billionaire investor Justin Sun is suing the family's World Liberty crypto venture after spending $45m on its tokens.

stories.title: Second French peacekeeper dies after ambush blamed on Hezbollah, Macron says
stories.summary: Established in 1978, UNIFIL has more than 10,000 peacekeepers from 50 countries who patrol the Blue Line, the UN-drawn border between Lebanon and Israel.

stories.title: Beijing artist marks World Earth Day with peace installation
stories.summary: Chinese artist Kong Ning marks World Earth Day in Beijing with a peace-themed installation, using wearable art to highlight environmental concerns.

stories.title: China weathered Trump's tariffs - but the Iran war is taking a toll
stories.summary: The Middle East conflict is putting pressure on factory orders, costs and jobs in China's export-driven economy.

stories.title: Girl, 14, found in singer D4vd's car died of 'multiple injuries'
stories.summary: The medical examiner said the 14-year-old's cause of death was determined months ago but was blocked from release.

stories.title: Meglepődött a HUN-REN a főigazgatók nyilatkozatán, ami szerint módosítanák a HUN-REN-törvényt
stories.summary: A szervezet vezetősége közleményt adott ki a napokban megjelent nyílt levélre: azt írják, nincs központosítás, de nyitottak arra, hogy az új kormányzattal a HUN-REN közfeladat-finanszírozási szerződést áttekintsék.

stories.title: A természet majd lerendezi
stories.summary: Öt éve a Tabánban hozták létre az első hazai Miyawaki-erdőt, azóta pedig bőven száz fölött van a számuk. Az apró oázisokban sokkal gyorsabban nőnek a fák, mert sűrűn ültetve egymással versenyeznek. A városi minierdők kiválóan mérséklik a klímaváltozás hatásait: csökkentik a hőmérsékletet, növelik a biodiverzitást, javítják a levegőminőséget és a közérzetünket is.

stories.title: Emberiesség elleni bűnök miatt állítják bíróság elé a Fülöp-szigetek volt elnökét
stories.summary: A Rodrigo Duterte ellen felhozott három vádpont a jogvédők szerint több ezer ember halálát okozta.

stories.title: Student allegedly jailed in China for six years after taking part in pro-democracy protests in Australia
stories.summary: Exclusive: The Australian government has been urged to take stronger action to protect overseas students from political repression The Australian government has been urged to take stronger action to protect Chinese international students from political repression by authorities on their return after a Chinese student was allegedly sentenced to six years’ imprisonment for joining pro-democracy protests in Australia. The student, who the Guardian has chosen not to name, lost contact with his frien

stories.title: Taiwan president blames China for forced cancellation of Eswatini trip
stories.summary: Lai Ching-te abandons visit after Seychelles, Mauritius and Madagascar revoke overflight permission Taiwan’s president, Lai Ching-te, has cancelled his trip to Eswatini, the democratic island’s only diplomatic ally in Africa, after his government said several countries had revoked overflight permits because of “intense pressure” from China. Lai was to leave on Wednesday for the 40th anniversary of King Mswati III’s accession. Continue reading...

stories.title: What course will Bulgaria’s new leader Rumen Radev take?
stories.summary: The trained pilot, former general and ex-president says he will crack down on corruption and seek dialogue with Russia. But who is Rumen Radev, the man who swept to victory in Sunday’s election?

stories.title: Russia and North Korea connect road bridge ahead of summer opening, Moscow says
stories.summary: Ties between both countries have surged amid Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine, with Moscow and Pyongyang deepening economic, political, cultural and military links.
